homeHero.Title: Konvikti
homeHero.subTitle: A home for theology students and other students in Kruununhaka and Ullanlinna
homeHead1.Title: A student residence in the heart of Helsinki.
homeHead1.Text: Konvikti has provided a home for theology students since 1917. Our mission is to offer a pleasant, affordable, and academically enriching place to live for the next hundred years of theology students—as well as for students interested in theology and Christianity.
homeHead1.Sub: Would you like to live in a historic setting right next to the university?
homeBrief2.Subtitle: In 1917, Herman Råbergh founded a support association for a home for poor and talented theology students, with the aim of supporting the studies of future generations of theologians. Our mission remains the same—we prioritize theology students and academic achievement in resident selections.
homeBrief.Subtitle: Konvikti in brief
homePunch1.Title: Founded in 1917
homePunch1.Text: In 1917, Herman Råbergh founded a support association for a home for poor and talented theology students, with the aim of supporting the studies of future generations of theologians. Our mission remains the same—we prioritize theology students and academic achievement in resident selections.
homePunch2.Title: A homelike environment...
homePunch2.Text: Konvikti is easy to arrive at and comfortable to live in. Compared to typical student dormitories, Konvikti offers both pleasant facilities and a warm, welcoming community.
homePunch3.Title: ...for theologians
homePunch3.Text: We prioritize theology students in resident selections. Beyond that, Konvikti residents don’t share any single background—people are interested in different areas of theology and come from different towns and congregations. Many have said that one of the great strengths of studying at Konvikti is this diversity.
homePunch4.Title: Easy living
homePunch4.Text: The student residence provides essentials such as cleaning supplies, well-equipped kitchens, and a washing machine. You only need furnishings for your own room—and even those can be available. Tidiness is maintained through weekly kitchen and apartment cleaning shifts.
homePunch5.Title: Over 100 years of student life—and counting
homePunch5.Text: Over the last hundred years, Konvikti has expanded to include the Pikku-Konvikti apartment in Ullanlinna, survived hits during the bombings of Helsinki, housed future archbishops and respected academics, and hosted countless sitsits and late-night scholarly conversations. The story continues through every Konvikti resident.
infoHero.Title: Apply to Konvikti
infoHero.subTitle: Use and help shape our technology
infoHead1.Title: Our student residences
infoHead1.Sub: Would you like to study in a beautiful historic setting just steps from the university? Explore Konvikti’s available resident places!
infoHead2.Title: Resident applications
infoHead2.Sub: New residents are selected annually through the general application period in June–August. We also hold additional application rounds as needed.
bigConvictusCard.title: Iso-Konvikti
smallConvictusCard.title: Pikku-Konvikti
infoBrief2.Subtitle: Applicants to Konvikti are evaluated using a points system. Those who receive the highest scores are selected as residents, as long as places are available. Students of theology are given priority.
infoBrief.Subtitle: Konvikti in Brief
infoPunch1.Title: Community
infoPunch1.Text: As a communal form of living, we value the willingness to take part in shared life — something that could simply be described as caring for others. You are interested in getting to know people and in taking care of small shared responsibilities. Your interest in community life is evaluated based on your motivation letter.
infoPunch2.Title: Interest in Theology and Christianity
infoPunch2.Text: Konvikti is a Christian student residence. Our residents come from many different backgrounds, but what unites us is an interest in theology and/or Christianity and church life. The academic, professional, or spiritual interest in theology or Christianity that you express will be evaluated based on your motivation letter. We do not assess the quality of that interest — only that it exists. Our hope is that Konvikti provides a formative environment, and it does so when students interested in the topic can meet others with diverse backgrounds.
infoPunch3.Title: Academic Achievement
infoPunch3.Text: Historically, Konvikti was founded as a 'student residence for talented but financially disadvantaged theology students.' For this reason, academic achievement is valued in the selection process. At the same time, our goal is to provide an academically enriching environment. Academic achievement is evaluated based on your matriculation certificate and school transcripts.
infoPunch4.Title: Am I a Suitable Applicant?
infoPunch4.Text: Are you about to begin your university studies, or already pursuing your first degree? Are you interested in Konvikti not only because of its location, but also because of the community and opportunities for academic or professional growth? Then you are part of our target group — do not hesitate to apply! We are not looking for a specific type of person, but for diverse students who are interested in theology and willing to contribute to the shared life of the community.
infoPunch5.Title: Community and Diverse Backgrounds as Strengths
infoPunch5.Text: Many have said that the best part of living at Konvikti has been meeting future theologians and others interested in the field from different backgrounds. Unlike most Christian student residences in Helsinki, Konvikti historically has no bonds to various factions inside the Church of Finland. Residents are not selected based on belonging to a particular church tradition or ideological background. What unites us is the community and a shared interest.
infoPunch6.Title: Optional Criteria – Income Level
infoPunch6.Text: Throughout our more than one hundred years of history, we have also given consideration in the selection process to applicants from modest financial backgrounds. Declaring your income level is voluntary and confidential, and may grant you an additional point in the application process.
infoHead3.Title: Resident application announcements
infoLastInfo.title: Were you selected?
infoLastInfo.sub: The warden will notify applicants of selections on the date specified in the application announcement. A place is granted to a theology student for six years and to students in other fields for three years. After that, the place can be renewed year by year.

join.title: Want to be part of Konvikti’s mission?
join.description: We are looking for people who are interested in supporting our work. Board roles, volunteer work, and other opportunities are waiting for you.

joinFinal.subtitle: Join us
joinFinal.title: Write your chapter in Konvikti’s story
joinFinal.description: As a supporting member of the association, you can help advance Konvikti’s values and goals with a small annual fee and in the way that suits you best. Whether you’re a Konvikti alumnus or simply interested in our work—our doors are open.

staff1.name: Samu-Ville Toivonen
staff1.title: Warden — Konvikti’s host
staff1.description: Samu-Ville holds a Bachelor’s degree in Theology and has served as Konvikti’s warden since 2026. The warden is responsible for the association’s administration and Konvikti’s day-to-day operations.

staff2.name: Benjam Bröijer
staff2.title: Chair of the Board
staff2.description: Benjam holds a Master’s degree in Theology and has served as Chair of the Board of the Konvikti Student Residence Support Association (registered association) since 2026. As Chair, he is responsible for leading the association and achieving its long-term goals.

value1.title: We will define Konvikti’s values in 2026.

goal1.title: Increase the number of residents
goal1.description: We will define Konvikti’s goals during 2026.

history1.title: Foundation
history1.description: The supporting association of the Konvikti Student Residence (Ylioppilaskoti Konvikti ry) was founded in 1917. Behind the founding were bishops of the Evangelical Lutheran Church of Finland, who wished to ensure that theology students would have a home and support in Helsinki.

history2.title: The First Konvikti
history2.description: In 1920 the student residence, Konvikti, was established. The word derives from the Latin term convictus, meaning communal life or living together in fellowship. The residence initially operated at several addresses: from 1920 to 1922 in Sörnäinen and Kalliola, and from 1922 to 1938 on Vuorikatu. Early wardens of the residence included Aarni Voipio and Aarre Lauha.

history3.title: Move to Meritullinkatu
history3.description: The residence moved to the current 'Great Konvikti' on Meritullinkatu in 1938. The original apartments were named Purgatory (Kiirastuli), Mirkkula, and Heaven (Taivas). Kiirastuli and Taivas remain part of the student residence today.

history4.title: The War Years
history4.description: As throughout the country, life at Konvikti during the war years was marked by scarcity, and most students were serving at the front. Three residents of Konvikti fell in the Winter War and five in the Continuation War. Their Pro Patria memorial plaques are still displayed in the library hall of Taivas. The top-floor apartment Taivas was destroyed in February 1944 during the major bombing of Helsinki. Even from this the community recovered — 'the place was just filled with brick mortar and debris for a while.'

history5.title: The Fourth Apartment – Manala
history5.description: In 1951 the supporting association purchased a fourth apartment for Konvikti in the same building. As the lowest apartment in the residence, located on the second floor, it received the name Manala ('hell').

history6.title: The Juurinen Student Residence – Little Konvikti
history6.description: Emil and Mimmi Juurinen bequeathed two apartments on Pieni Roobertinkatu to the residence. This donation became known as Pikku-Konvikti, the 'Little Konvikti'.

history7.title: Jukola (1999–2024)
history7.description: A former temperance residence located in the same stairwell at Meritullinkatu 22 was transferred to Konvikti's use. It was named Jukola after its seven—and later eight—resident places - a reference to the Finnish great writer Aleksis Kivi's novel 'The Seven Brothers.'

history8.title: Letting Go of Jukola and Mirkkula
history8.description: Due to rising costs, the association eventually had to give up both the rented apartment Jukola and the Mirkkula shares. This financial restructuring has secured the stability of the residence for decades to come.

history9.title: A New Era
history9.description: Now over a century old, Konvikti looks toward the future: how can it best serve the purpose of its founders, the homeland, and the Christian church of Finland?
